inputs.label: standard
inputs.statistic: The odds ratio for developing Type 2 diabetes among participants with a BMI over 30 was 3.2 (95% CI: 2.7-3.8, p < 0.001) compared to participants with a BMI under 25.
inputs.context: A 10-year longitudinal study of 45,000 adults. Baseline diabetes prevalence in the normal-BMI group was 4.5% over the study period.

inputs.label: minimal
inputs.statistic: Median household income rose 2.3% in nominal terms but fell 1.1% in real terms after adjusting for inflation.
inputs.context: Annual economic report covering the past fiscal year.

inputs.label: european
inputs.statistic: The Gini coefficient for income inequality in the EU rose from 0.297 in 2020 to 0.312 in 2025, with the largest increases in countries that experienced the highest post-pandemic inflation.
inputs.context: Eurostat annual income distribution report covering all 27 EU member states. The EU's Gini coefficient has historically ranged between 0.28 and 0.31 over the past two decades.
